data.name: CtrfReportArtifactDescription
data.value: CTRF (Common Test Report Format) JSON test report

data.name: CtrfReportFileExistsAndWillBeOverwritten
data.value: Warning: CTRF report file '{0}' already exists and will be overwritten.

data.name: CtrfReportFileNameExtensionIsNotJson
data.value: '--report-ctrf-filename' file name argument must end with '.json' (e.g. --report-ctrf-filename myreport.ctrf.json)

data.name: CtrfReportFileNameMustNotBeEmpty
data.value: '--report-ctrf-filename' file name part must not be empty (e.g. --report-ctrf-filename myreport.ctrf.json)

data.name: CtrfReportFileNameOptionDescription
data.value: The name of the generated CTRF report. May include a relative or absolute path; relative paths are resolved against the test results directory and missing directories are created. Supports the following placeholders: {pname} (test application name), {pid} (process ID), {asm} (entry assembly name), {tfm} (target framework moniker), {time} (timestamp). Example: MyReport_{tfm}.ctrf.json

data.name: CtrfReportFileNameRelativePathMustStayUnderResultsDirectory
data.value: '--report-ctrf-filename' relative paths must stay under the test results directory (e.g. --report-ctrf-filename nested/myreport.ctrf.json)

data.name: CtrfReportGeneratorDescription
data.value: Produce a CTRF (Common Test Report Format) JSON report for the current test session (https://ctrf.io)

data.name: CtrfReportOptionDescription
data.value: Enable generating a CTRF (Common Test Report Format) JSON report

data.name: InvalidTestApplicationBuilderType
data.value: CTRF report generator only works with builders of type 'Microsoft.Testing.Platform.Builder.TestApplicationBuilder'
